methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Sublime Systems is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Leah Ellis and Yet-Ming Chiang that has raised $80M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 comparable companies—1 public and 8 private—operating in the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total capital raised as a proxy baseline, applies a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier to the $80M raised, implying a valuation of $152M. Peer-median benchmarking across 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $355M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital intensity within climate-tech.

The two-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $129M (low) to $408M (high), with a median of $254M. Stage-progression alone suggests the lower bound; peer median dominates the upper end. Key uncertainties include Sublime Systems' relative technology differentiation versus Commonwealth Fusion Systems and Pano AI, both valued substantially above the peer median, and the degree to which the $5.0B outlier reflects sectoral tailwinds or company-specific factors. Further clarity on capital efficiency and go-to-market traction would narrow this range.
